author: Nate Miller
title: Brandan Bidois sent to minors
desc: Brandon Bidois optioned back to Triple-A Indianapolis.
impact: With Antwone Kelly called up ahead of Friday's game against the Marlins, Bidois will head back to the minors. Bidois made a dozen appearances for the Pirates over the last month, posting a 4.05 ERA and 16-11 K/BB ratio across 13 1/3 innings.

author: Nate Miller
title: Antwone Kelly called up Friday
desc: Antwone Kelly was called up from Triple-A ahead of Friday's game against the Marlins.
impact: The Pirates No. 7 prospect per MLB.com, Kelly will make his first trip up to the show on Friday. The 22-year-old righty features a fastball that can reach triple digits, and he's posted a 4.50 ERA with a 47-24 K/BB ratio across 54 innings for Triple-A Indianapolis so far this season. Of Kelly's 13 appearances this year, 10 have been as a starter. However, he is expected to work out of the Pirates' bullpen.

author: Nate Miller
title: Daniel Espino to be called up Friday
desc: Daniel Espino will be called up from Triple-A Columbus ahead of Friday's game against the Tigers.
impact: A first-round pick all the way back in 2019 and a highly touted prospect as recently as 2023, Espino has battled through multiple injuries over the past few years to get to this point. The 25-year-old righty has posted an elevated 5.30 ERA over 18 2/3 innings at Triple-A Columbus this year, but a 3.44 xERA is more encouraging. Walks (15) have been an issue, but he's countered them with 29 strikeouts.

author: Nate Miller
title: Jordan Lawlar to join Diamondbacks on Friday
desc: Jordan Lawlar will be activated from the IL and join the Diamondbacks ahead of Friday's game against the Reds.
impact: Lawlar was off to a hot start for the Diamondbacks this season before a wrist injury put him on the shelf for the past couple of months, going 6-for-18 with a double, a walk, and a steal over the first six games. He is now ready to rejoin the club, and his versatility should keep him in the lineup regularly.

author: Nate Miller
title: Derek Hill sent to Philadelphia
desc: Derek Hill was dealt to the Phillies on Thursday.
impact: The Phillies were certainly in need of some outfield depth after Adolis Garcia (shoulder) went down with an injury Wednesday, so the front office wasted no time in using an off day to acquire some. Hill can play all three spots in the outfield, and despite batting just .213 so far this season, he brings a decent power-speed element, with four homers and seven steals over 90 plate appearances for the White Sox.

author: Bruno Mulé
title: Kumar Rocker exits in fifth for first time since May 8
desc: Rocker has a 3.56 ERA, 1.34 WHIP, and 52:28 K:BB across 65.2 innings over 13 appearances this season. Thursday marked the first time since May 8 that he failed to complete five innings, ending a stretch of consistent length.
impact: He held Kansas City scoreless on one hit through three frames before allowing two runs on three hits and a walk in the fourth. Rocker retired the first two batters in the fifth before a Bobby Witt single prompted his exit, finishing with three strikeouts across 4.2 innings in the no-decision.

author: Bruno Mulé
title: Corey Seager homers twice in five games since IL return
desc: Seager is slashing .186/.284/.373 across 204 plate appearances this season and has gone 5-for-21 with two home runs and four RBI over five games since returning from the injured list on June 5.
impact: His 391-foot solo shot in the third inning Thursday was the only home run in the game. Seager finished 2-for-4 with a run scored in the 4-2 win over Kansas City.

author: Bruno Mulé
title: Bo Bichette homers after 0-for-8 series start
desc: Bichette is slashing .227/.277/.330 with 34 RBI and 31 runs scored across 296 plate appearances this season but has gone 10-for-29 with a .920 OPS over his last seven games. He had gone 0-for-8 in the first two games of the Cardinals series before hitting a two-run homer off Hunter Dobbins in the first inning Thursday to put the Mets ahead 2-1.
impact: Bichette finished 1-for-4 with his sixth homer of the season in the 5-4 win.

author: Bruno Mulé
title: Jared Young posts .911 OPS since returning from IL
desc: Since coming off the injured list on May 26, Young has gone 12-for-46 with a .911 OPS while playing nearly every day at first base. He tied the game 4-4 with an RBI single in the fifth inning Thursday before the Mets held on for the win.
impact: Young had extended New York's lead to 3-1 with his fourth homer of the season off Hunter Dobbins in the first inning, finishing 2-for-4 with two RBI in the 5-4 victory over St. Louis.

author: Nate Miller
title: Nolan Jones dealt to White Sox
desc: Nolan Jones was traded to the White Sox on Thursday.
impact: Jones has yet to play in the majors this season after posting a mere .600 OPS with five homers and eight steals in over 400 plate appearances with the Guardians in 2025. Jones has been productive for Triple-A Columbus this year, putting up a .275/.385/.460 slash line with eight home runs and 31 RBI over 52 games. He will initially report to that same level in the White Sox system.

author: Nate Miller
title: Tommy Pham to opt of contract
desc: Tommy Pham has decided to opt out of his deal with the Orioles.
impact: Pham signed a minor-league contract with the Orioles in mid-May and has been showing plenty of pop in his bat recently. That still has not resulted in a call-up, so the 38-year-old veteran has decided to try the open market once again.

author: Bruno Mulé
title: Juan Soto snaps 1-for-17 skid with go-ahead homer
desc: Soto is slashing .277/.369/.537 with 31 RBI, 30 runs scored, and six stolen bases across 218 plate appearances this season. He had gone 1-for-17 over his previous five games before breaking a 4-4 tie in the seventh inning Thursday with his 14th homer - a 398-foot shot off a JoJo Romero two-strike sweeper.
impact: Soto finished 2-for-4 with a double and two runs scored in the 5-4 win over St. Louis.

author: Bruno Mulé
title: Christian Scott allows three homers after 29.2-inning HR-free streak
desc: Scott has a 3.10 ERA, 1.35 WHIP, and 47:19 K:BB across 40.2 innings this season and faces Cincinnati on the road in his next start. He had allowed just one home run all year and none in his last six starts covering 29.2 innings before Thursday against St. Louis.
impact: Three home runs in the first two innings accounted for all four runs Scott allowed. He struck out six across 4.2 innings and did not factor into the decision in the Mets' 5-4 win.

author: Bruno Mulé
title: Devin Williams converts seventh straight save chance
desc: Williams has a 5.32 ERA, 1.50 WHIP, and 35:14 K:BB across 22 innings this season but has been scoreless in 14 of his last 16 appearances and has converted each of his last seven save chances. Thursday marked his ninth save of the year.
impact: He struck out two in a perfect ninth inning to close out the Mets' 5-4 win over St. Louis.

author: Nate Miller
title: Martin Perez next start moved to Saturday
desc: Martin Perez will not start until Saturday against the Mets.
impact: Perez was slated to matchup with the White Sox on Thursday, but that game was postponed. The veteran lefty will now be bumped back to Saturday, as Spencer Strider will take the mound for the Braves on Friday.
